summary: Iteration 44 — Verified the user-reported curriculum sidebar bug. The SideMenu.tsx fix (adding PERMISSIONS.VIEW_CURRICULUM to the 'curriculum' item permissions array) is CORRECT but DOES NOT manifest in the UI because of a deeper bug: for non-admin users, the SideMenu shows ONLY the home item ('الرئيسية'). ALL permission-gated items are filtered out for view_curr_user even though localStorage.user.permissions DOES include the required permissions (view_curriculum, manage_students, view_attendance, view_lectures, etc.). The control test as admin works correctly (all expected items show). This is a regression in AuthContext's hasAnyPermission/permission propagation — not in the SideMenu MENU_ITEMS configuration.

minor.issue: Login as view_curr_user returns 200 with permissions list including 'view_curriculum' — confirmed correct
One 403 console error after login as view_curr_user (resource not identified — likely an endpoint where view_curr_user lacks permission such as /api/teachers or /api/courses). Non-fatal.

ui_bugs.issue: For non-admin user 'view_curr_user' (role=employee), the side menu shows ONLY 'الرئيسية'. Expected items based on permissions: 'إدارة الحضور' (view_attendance), 'إدارة الطلاب' (manage_students/view_students), 'الخطة الدراسية' (view_curriculum), 'لوحة الإدارة' (manage_students), 'الخريجون' (view_students). ALL are missing.

User permissions are correctly stored in localStorage.user.permissions and useAuthStore (header shows correct name/role 'موظف'), but hasAnyPermission from useAuth() returns false for all matching permissions. Likely cause: AuthContext's React user state is null at the time SideMenu renders, and hasAnyPermission (unlike hasPermission at AuthContext.tsx:204-213) does NOT have a localStorage fallback for reading user. This produces filter=false → items hidden.

integration_issues.issue: Login via authStore writes user to localStorage and updates useAuthStore.user, but AuthContext.user state may not be in sync. hasAnyPermission has no localStorage fallback, so it returns false → curriculum (and every other permission-gated) menu item is hidden.

verifications_performed: POST /api/auth/login (view_curr_user/test1234) → 200, permissions list includes 'view_curriculum' and other employee defaults — PASS
localStorage after login contains user.permissions with 'view_curriculum' — PASS
Admin (admin/admin123) → side menu shows 'الخطة الدراسية' (PRESENT) and all other items — PASS (control)
view_curr_user (employee with view_curriculum) → side menu shows ONLY 'الرئيسية'; 'الخطة الدراسية' MISSING (and so are 'إدارة الطلاب', 'إدارة الحضور', 'الخريجون', 'لوحة الإدارة') — FAIL

Manual filter simulation in browser using localStorage.user.permissions correctly returns hasAny=true for curriculum/students/admin/manage-attendance items — confirming the data is correct and the bug is purely in React state / hasAnyPermission logic
rca_of_the_issue: The SideMenu.tsx change (lines 50-54) that added PERMISSIONS.VIEW_CURRICULUM to the curriculum item's permissions array is CORRECT. However, the user cannot observe the fix because of a pre-existing regression in /app/frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx: hasAnyPermission (line 234) and hasAllPermissions (line 246) only consult `cachedUser || user`. If the React user state hasn't been hydrated yet (or login flow only updates Zustand authStore + localStorage, not AuthContext), and module-level cachedUser is null, these methods return false (line 238 / 250). Unlike hasPermission (line 204-213), they do NOT read localStorage as a fallback. Net effect: all permission-gated menu items are hidden for non-admin users. The home item appears only because it has forAll:true and bypasses hasAnyPermission. Reproduction: 1) Clear localStorage. 2) Login as view_curr_user / test1234. 3) Click the floating-menu-btn. 4) Observe only 'الرئيسية' is visible. Mitigation options for main agent: (a) Mirror the localStorage fallback from hasPermission into hasAnyPermission and hasAllPermissions, OR (b) Ensure the authStore login also calls AuthContext.login() so the React state user is populated, OR (c) Change SideMenu.tsx to read user/permissions from useAuthStore directly and implement the hasAny check inline instead of via useAuth().
action_items: [CRITICAL] Fix AuthContext.hasAnyPermission and hasAllPermissions to add localStorage fallback (same pattern as hasPermission at lines 204-213), OR sync AuthContext.user state with authStore on login. Without this, the curriculum sidebar fix is invisible to the user.
[CRITICAL] Re-test after fix: login as view_curr_user → open side menu → confirm 'الخطة الدراسية' appears → click it → confirm /curriculum page loads with no 403.
[LOW] Investigate the 403 console error after login (some endpoint is being called that view_curr_user lacks permission for).
critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext.tsx: hasPermission has a localStorage fallback (lines 204-213) but hasAnyPermission (234-243) and hasAllPermissions (246-254) do NOT. This inconsistency is the root cause of the side menu bug. Either all three should have the same fallback or none should — current state is inconsistent.
Module-level mutable state `let cachedUser` and `let cachedToken` (AuthContext.tsx:96-97) is risky and can carry stale data across logins; consider removing in favor of consistent state hydration.

SideMenu.tsx mixes two state sources: `useAuthStore((s)=>s.user)` for user display and `useAuth()` for permission checks. If these two sources can diverge (e.g., one populated, the other not), permission checks are unreliable. Recommend a single source of truth.
SideMenu.tsx: MenuItem interface (line 18-26) doesn't declare 'teacherOnly' but it's used in MENU_ITEMS (line 38) and the filter (line 158) — TS would warn if strict. Minor.
MENU_ITEMS curriculum permissions array correctly contains both MANAGE_CURRICULUM and VIEW_CURRICULUM — the fix is in place at the data layer.

success_rate.frontend: 0% on bug fix verification — sidebar still hides curriculum link for view_curr_user

test_credentials: admin/admin123 (works correctly); view_curr_user/test1234 (login works, permissions correct in API, but UI menu broken)
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent applies fix (localStorage fallback in hasAnyPermission or sync AuthContext.user with authStore login), re-run the flow: login as view_curr_user / test1234, open the floating menu (data-testid='floating-menu-btn'), and assert 'الخطة الدراسية' appears in the menu list, then click it and assert URL contains '/curriculum' and no 403/forbidden text appears. Admin path is already confirmed working and is a good control. The SideMenu.tsx MENU_ITEMS data is already correct — do not modify it.
